span.p: ACH / SEPA / bank transfer: delayed settlement 1-5 days

span.p: How many PSPs / payment methods do you genuinely need now?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Single PSP integration - simplest; abstract behind your OWN payment interface so a later swap is bounded

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the need real today or speculative?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Orchestration layer: route by method/region/cost, normalize webhooks + tokens behind one internal model

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: What is the settlement timing of the chosen method?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Authorized != settled - is auth enough to fulfill your risk?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Fulfill on authorized; reconcile settlement later

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Gate fulfillment on the captured/settled webhook, not just auth

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Do NOT fulfill on initiation - settlement can fail/return days later; gate fulfillment on the cleared/settled webhook

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Drive every state transition from VERIFIED webhooks; post to the double-entry ledger
